title: Within-Lap Ranker Sidecar
summary: ROC AUC rewards ordering, and pit calls are often relative to nearby rivals rather than absolute thresholds.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ordering, and pit calls are often relative to nearby rivals rather than absolute thresholds. Official F1 strategy material emphasizes undercut, cover-stop, clean air, and pit-window timing, which are inherently within-lap competitive comparisons. A grouped ranker can learn who is most likely to pit next among cars on the same lap/race even if absolute probabilities are noisy.
implementation_hint: Create per-(Year,Race,LapNumber) groups, add field-relative features such as tyre-life percentile, lap-delta percentile, degradation percentile, position bucket, distance to median field state, and nearest-neighbor gaps by position. Train an LGBMRanker with group ids equal to same-lap race groups, then blend its rank-normalized OOF scores with the current binary model.
expected_effect: Better local ordering among borderline pit-window rows; likely small but real AUC lift if many errors are pairwise swaps within the same race state.
risk: Groups are small and noisy; if labels are sparse on many laps, the ranker may overfit or just reproduce the base model.
